experiment: behavioral-contract vs types-only, parallel multi-agent build of one codebase
independent_variable: Arm A agents received BEHAVIOR.md (seam-ownership + runtime semantics); Arm B received types-only. Both received identical SPEC.md + constitution + shared types.ts.

P0_happy_path.note: committer gates staging on autoAdd (default false) -> stages nothing -> refuses 'nothing staged'

P6_hook_failure.note: VACUOUS pass: hook never ran; commit refused earlier by the staging break (cascade from P0)

armB.score: 2 genuine pass (P2,P5); 2 hard fail (P0,P1); 1 vacuous pass (P6)

redaction.armA: messager only (daemon passes raw, single owner)
redaction.armB: DUPLICATED: daemon AND messager both redact, each citing the other as harmless backstop

merge_pause_guard.armA: committer authoritative + daemon early + approval defensive

staging.armB: committer stages only if config.autoAdd (default false) -> no-op

proximate_causes.P1_leak: Both Arm B redactors use sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{16,} (hyphen NOT in char class) and lack JWT + generic-blob rules. Arm A uses sk-[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{16,} (hyphen IN class) + JWT rule + 40+ char blob catch-all.
proximate_causes.P0_nocommit: Arm B committer: `if (config.autoAdd && draft.files.length) git add ...` else stage nothing, then `git diff --cached --quiet` -> refuse if empty. autoAdd defaults false; probes never pre-stage -> always refuses.
